data.title: Over 90 crypto ETFs await SEC approval, with XRP and Solana leading. SEC flooded with applications post Bitcoin, Ethereum nod.
data.description: Following the approval of Bitcoin and Ethereum products by the SEC, the regulatory body is now inundated with an increasing number of applications for cryptocurrency ETFs. The SEC's decision to give the green light to these products has spiked interest in the market, with a significant uptick seen in the number of applications for crypto-related exchange-traded funds. This signifies a shifting trend towards mainstream acceptance and adoption of digital assets, with regulatory authorities now grappling with the task of evaluating and processing this surge in requests. The surge in applications is indicative of the growing demand and interest in crypto investments, with the SEC now tasked with navigating this evolving landscape to ensure compliance and investor protection.

data.title: US Army partners with Striveworks, using AI to enhance protection along Southern Border.
data.description: In Austin, Texas, the U.S. Army's JTF-Southern Border is utilizing Striveworks' AIOps platform to enhance persistent and actionable domain awareness. This partnership incorporates advanced technology to bolster intelligence gathering and decision-making capabilities along the southern border of the United States.

data.title: Bitcoin falls below $109,000 and XRP below $3. ETH at $4,350.55. 144,840 traders liquidated for $545.67 million in past 24 hours.
data.description: Cryptocurrency markets are experiencing a decline as the holiday weekend approaches. The current prices for popular cryptocurrencies are as follows: Bitcoin at $108,506.34, Ethereum at $4,350.55, Solana at $203.15, XRP at $2.82, Dogecoin at $0.2118, and Shiba Inu at $0.00001204. Recent data reveals that 144,840 traders were liquidated in the past day, resulting in losses totaling $545.67 million. Additionally, there were net inflows of $178.9 million into spot Bitcoin ETFs and $39.2 million into spot Ethereum ETFs on Wednesday. For more detailed information, the full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Trump urges Powell to consider government's rising debt service costs at upcoming Fed meeting.
data.description: President Donald Trump is urging Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell to take into account the rising cost of the government's debt service payments during the central bank's upcoming meeting. This comes as the government's debt has been increasing steadily, with debt service payments now reaching a significant amount. Trump's request highlights the economic challenges posed by the growing national debt and emphasizes the need for the Federal Reserve to consider this factor in its decision-making process. The issue is set to be discussed as the central bank convenes later this month.

data.title: PennyMac Financial to present at 2025 Barclays Conference with CEO David Spector and CFO Daniel Perotti at 2:45pm EST.
data.description: PennyMac Financial Services, Inc. (NYSE: PFSI) from Westlake Village, California, disclosed plans to co-present with PennyMac Mortgage Investment Trust (NYSE: PMT) at the 2025 Barclays Global Financial Services Conference. The event is set for Monday, September 8, 2025, at 2:45 p.m. Eastern Time. The discussion will feature Chairman and CEO David Spector and CFO Daniel Perotti. The presentation will be in the format of a fireside chat. For those interested, a live audio webcast link will be provided.

data.title: American Nurses Association concerned by high number of sudden leadership changes at CDC, impacting public health.
data.description: A concerning number of leadership transitions poses potential risks to public health. The vital link between public health, the economy, and national security should not be underestimated. The American Nurses Association (ANA) from Silver Spring, Md., revealed on August 29, 2025, the urgency of addressing this issue. It highlighted the significant impact on the nation's well-being and emphasized the importance of stable leadership to ensure effective management in this critical sector.

data.title: Verb Technology (Nasdaq: VERB) to release Chairman's message on Sep 5 after renaming to TON Strategy Company.
data.description: In Las Vegas, Verb Technology Company, Inc. (Nasdaq: VERB) revealed plans to release its inaugural "Chairman’s Message" on September 5, 2025. Manuel Stotz, the Executive Chairman of the Board of Directors, will issue the message to offer stockholders and the wider market a glimpse into the Company's strategy, advancements, and future outlook. The Company, formerly known as Verb Techn, has informed the market about its upcoming name change.
